evidence: grep -qE 'curl\s+.*\|\s*(ba)?sh' is the hook's own PreToolUse blocklist pattern text, never executed as a command; the hook exits 2 to block matching Bash calls
description: Critical pattern match is the literal detection regex inside a defensive hook, not a live curl-pipe-sh execution
fp_reason: The matched text is the pattern string a security-blocking hook uses to detect and reject curl-pipe-sh commands, not an execution of one
rule_gap: Naive text-based pre-scans should exclude matches that occur inside grep/regex pattern literals within files whose own purpose is to detect that same pattern (e.g. PreToolUse blocklist hooks)

evidence: grep -qE 'wget\s+.*\|\s*(ba)?sh' is the hook's own PreToolUse blocklist pattern text, never executed as a command; the hook exits 2 to block matching Bash calls
description: Critical pattern match is the literal detection regex inside a defensive hook, not a live wget-pipe-sh execution
fp_reason: The matched text is the pattern string a security-blocking hook uses to detect and reject wget-pipe-sh commands, not an execution of one
rule_gap: Naive text-based pre-scans should exclude matches that occur inside grep/regex pattern literals within files whose own purpose is to detect that same pattern (e.g. PreToolUse blocklist hooks)
